task: Write a clear 2-3 sentence explanation, for a non-expert, of how an on-chain escrow lets two agents who don't trust each other transact safely.

client_fund.reason: The task is legitimate and the price of 10 USDC is well within the remaining budget of 1000 USDC.

evaluate.reason: The deliverable provides a clear, non-expert explanation of on-chain escrow in exactly three sentences, covering the smart contract, conditional release, and trustless safety via blockchain.

deliverable: An on-chain escrow uses a smart contract—a self-executing program on a blockchain—to hold funds from one party until both parties fulfill their agreed-upon conditions. The contract automatically releases the payment to the seller only when the buyer confirms receipt or a set rule is met, so neither side can cheat. This way, two agents who don’t trust each other can transact safely because the blockchain acts as a neutral, tamper‑proof intermediary.
